chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA Cr.B.A.No.S-164 of 2020 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For hearing of bail application. 02.04.2020. Mr. Muhammad Afzal Jagirani, advocate for applicants. Mr. Aitbar Ali Bullo, D.P.G for the State. -.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Irshad Ali Shah J;- It is alleged that the applicants were found manufacturing “Katcha” wine and then made their escape good from the place of incident, for that the present case was registered. 2. The applicants on having been refused pre- arrest bail by learned 2nd Additional Sessions Judge, Jacobabad, have sought for the same from this Court by way of instant application u/s 498 Cr.P.C. 3. It is contended by learned counsel for the applicants that they being innocent have been involved in this case falsely by the police otherwise they have nothing to do with the alleged incident; the case has finally been challaned and the offence is not falling within the prohibitory clause of section 497 Cr.PC. By contending so, he sought for pre-arrest bail for the applicants on point of malafide. 4. Learned D.P.G. for the State has opposed to grant of pre arrest bail to the applicants by contending that the alleged offence is affecting the society at large. 5. I have considered the above arguments and perused the record. 6. There is no independent witness to the incident. The alleged offence is not falling within the prohibitory clause of Section 497 Cr.PC. The alleged recovery has been subjected to chemical examination with considerable delay. The investigation of the case is over and there is no chance of tampering with the evidence on part of applicants, as all the witnesses are police officials.

The alleged recovery has been subjected to chemical examination with considerable delay. The investigation of the case is over and there is no chance of tampering with the evidence on part of applicants, as all the witnesses are police officials. In these circumstances, the applicants are found entitled to grant of pre-arrest bail on point of malafide. 7. In view of above, the interim pre-arrest bail already granted to applicants is confirmed on same terms and conditions. 8. The instant bail application is disposed of in above terms. J U D G E
